dbs_disclosure_and_barring_service.text: Non-departmental public body of Home Office providing access to criminal record information
dbs_disclosure_and_barring_service.predecessor_merger: Criminal Records Bureau (CRB) and Independent Safeguarding Authority (ISA)

enhanced_disclosure_and_barring_service.text: Shows both spent and unspent convictions and cautions

basic_criminal_disclosure.text: Shows unspent convictions according to Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974

dvla_driver_and_vehicle_licensing_agency.text: Executive agency of Department for Transport

responsibilities.text: Maintains registers of drivers and vehicles
Facilitates road safety and general law enforcement

dvsa_driver_and_vehicle_standards_author.text: Provides licensing, testing, and enforcement services

responsibilities.text: Ensures compliance of operators and drivers

dft_department_for_transport.text: Determines overall transport strategy for UK

licensing_authority.text: Authority responsible for issuing licences for Hackney Carriage and Private Hire drivers, vehicles, and operators

operates_under.text: Part II Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1976

licensing_officer.text: Council Officer authorised to act in accordance with Hackney Carriage and Private Hire legislation

licensing_authority_vehicle_test_certifi.text: Vehicle test undertaken by approved garages within controlled district

includes.text: Verification vehicle meets Licensing Authority conditions (roof signs, plates, etc.)

medical_group_2.text: Medical examination to Group 2 standards set by DVLA

knowledge_test_hackney_carriage.text: Examination of knowledge regarding rules, regulations, and topographical knowledge of licensing area

literacy_and_numeracy_test_private_hire.text: Covers basic map reading, Rule Book contents, fare calculations, Road Traffic Regulations
literacy_and_numeracy_test_private_hire.after_3_failures: must demonstrate improved skills through Adult Education Department

single_vehicle_approval_certificate_sva.text: Pre-registration inspection for cars and light goods vehicles not type-approved to British or European standards

applies_to.text: Vehicles designed and constructed to modern safety and environmental standards

controlled_district.text: Area covered by Gloucester City Council Licensing Authority

hackney_carriage_taxi.text: Wheeled vehicle constructed or adapted to seat no more than 8 passengers

characteristics.text: Carries passengers for hire and reward
May ply for hire in any street within licensed district

private_hire.text: Provision of vehicle constructed or adapted to seat no more than 8 passengers by licensed private hire operator

characteristics.text: Cannot be provided at taxi stands

taxi_rank_stand.text: Approved rank within controlled district where taxis can await arrival of hirer

premises_licence.text: Licence issued by Licensing Authority under Licensing Act 2003
premises_licence.permits: licensable activities including sale by retail of alcohol

temporary_event_notice.text: Notice submitted to Licensing Authority under Licensing Act 2003
temporary_event_notice.relates_to: licensable activities including sale by retail of alcohol

london_type_hackney_carriage.text: Purpose-built hackney carriage recognisable by public as taxi

sub_contracting.text: Private Hire operator takes booking and arranges for another licensed operator to make journey

metadata.content_category: Hackney Carriage & Private Hire Licensing Guidance

page.summary: Online applications and forms for taxi licensing services
page.title: Online Forms and Applications

applications.description: New Hackney Carriage and Private Hire driver
Renew Hackney Carriage and Private Hire driver
New Hackney Carriage and Private Hire vehicle
Renew Hackney Carriage and Private Hire vehicle
Apply for private hire vehicle plate exemption as chauffeur
Notify licensing team of address change on licence
Apply for a change of vehicle licence
Apply to Transfer the Ownership of a Vehicle Licence
Report a Hackney Carriage or Private Hire accident

page.summary: Mandatory standards for hackney carriage and private hire vehicles applying for licence or renewal
page.title: Vehicle Eligibility Requirements

age_and_size.text: Vehicle must not exceed 7 years from date of first registration

emissions_and_standards.text: Vehicle must meet Euro 6 emission standards

hackney_carriage_specific.text: Gloucester City taxi roof sign must be displayed
Blue licence plates must be displayed in window and on rear
Taximeter must be installed and checked at approved testing station
Current tariff card must be displayed in vehicle

mandatory_equipment.text: First aid kit must be carried
No Smoking stickers must be displayed
Licence plates must be displayed showing licence number, vehicle details, and licence expiry date

private_hire_specific.text: Yellow bus lane sticker must be displayed
Stickers indicating no insurance if not pre-booked must be displayed
Yellow licence plates must be displayed in window and on front and rear

general.text: Fares must be charged according to taximeter tariff regulated by council
Licence issued for 12 months at a time
Annual inspection required at approved testing station

hackney_carriage_operations.text: Vehicle may be hailed in street
Vehicle may use Gloucester taxi ranks

private_hire_operations.text: Fares must be pre-booked through licensed Gloucester private hire operator
Vehicle must not use taxi ranks
Vehicle must not use roof sign

insurance.text: Insurance must cover hire and reward
Applicant/vehicle proprietor must be named driver on policy
If fleet insurance: insurance certificate and schedule of vehicles required

vehicle_inspection.text: Licensing Authority may check comfort and cleanliness
Licensing Authority may check door safety and security
Vehicle inspection certificate required on application or renewal
Meter inspection certificate required if meter installed

new_application.text: V5 registration document or new keepers supplement if recently purchased
MOT certificate (not required if vehicle less than 1 year old)
Meter inspection certificate if meter installed
If fleet insurance: insurance certificate and schedule of vehicles

renewal_application.text: Renewal letter and vehicle licence reference number
Meter inspection certificate (not required if previously provided and tariff unchanged)
If fleet insurance: insurance certificate and schedule of vehicles

renewal_vehicle_change.text: Meter inspection certificate if meter installed
New keepers supplement if recently purchased
MOT certificate (not required if vehicle less than 1 year old)

transfer_of_ownership.text: Proof of ownership: letter from previous owner stating no longer using vehicle
V5 registration document or new keepers supplement if recently purchased
MOT certificate (not required if vehicle less than 1 year old)
Basic DBS certificate less than one month old (unless applicant is licensed driver with Gloucester City Council)
If fleet insurance: insurance certificate and schedule of vehicles

decision_timeline.text: Decision may take longer in some cases

page.summary: Requirements and process for transferring hackney carriage or private hire vehicle licence to new proprietor
page.title: Transfer of Vehicle Licence Ownership

hackney_carriage_transfer.text: Existing licensed hackney carriage vehicles transferable to new proprietor
Licence can continue for saloon or estate type vehicles
All new additional hackney carriage vehicles must be wheelchair accessible

transfer_eligibility.text: Vehicle must not exceed 15 years from date of first registration
Vehicle must meet Euro 6 emission standards
Fares charged according to taximeter tariff
Licensed for 12 months at a time
Annual inspection at approved testing station required

page.summary: Services available for changes to vehicle registration, plate exemptions, replacements, and address changes
page.title: Minor Vehicle Licence Applications

services.description: Apply for private hire vehicle plate exemption as chauffeur
Notify licensing team of address change on licence

page.summary: Mandatory reporting requirements for accidents affecting vehicle safety, performance, or appearance
page.title: Vehicle Accident Reporting

reporting_obligations.text: Accidents affecting vehicle safety, performance, or appearance must be reported
Report must be submitted within 72 hours of accident

scope.text: Requirement applies to hackney carriage vehicles
Requirement applies to private hire vehicles

page.summary: Mandatory requirements and processes for hackney carriage and private hire driver licensing
page.title: Driver Licensing Requirements

age_and_experience.text: Valid UK driving licence required (not provisional)
Driving licence must have been valid for minimum 12 continuous months immediately prior to application

suitability_assessment.text: Applicant must be assessed as fit and proper person
Applicants should have no more than 3 current penalty points on DVLA licence
Standard test: would you allow person for whom you care to travel alone in vehicle at any time of day or night
If answer is no on balance of probabilities, individual must not hold licence
Overriding consideration is safeguarding the public
Applicants must not be given the benefit of doubt
If decision-maker is 50/50 on fitness, applicant must not hold licence
Threshold is lower than criminal conviction standard
Can consider conduct not resulting in criminal conviction
Assessment includes character and relevant information relating to current or previous history

mandatory_documentation.text: Photograph: light background, full face uncovered, no sunglasses (head coverings allowed for religious reasons)
Current valid DVLA driving licence showing home address (photocard or pre-1998 paper licence)
Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) Enhanced Criminal Convictions Certificate
Group 2 medical certificate from GP no more than 3 months old
Driving Assessment pass certificate from Council approved provider (Diamond Advance Test, Green Penny, The Blue Lamp Trust or equivalent)
Right to work check under Immigration Act 2016
English proficiency test pass or equivalent educational qualification

dbs_requirements.text: DBS certificate must specify Other Workforce
DBS certificate must include Child and Adult barring list information
Must be registered for DBS Online Update Service prior to licensing
DBS certificate less than one month old required at application
Licence unlikely to be granted if applicant appears on either barred list
Driver responsible for maintaining active subscription and card details
DBS subscription lapse results in licence suspension until new certificate issued and verified
Certificate used for Online Update Service registration must be retained by licence holder for inspection on request

medical_certificate.text: Group 2 medical certificate from GP required
Medical certificate must be no more than 3 months old
Group 2 standards same as public service vehicles and heavy goods vehicle drivers

driving_assessment.text: Assessment must be from Council approved provider

wheelchair_accessible_vehicle_drivers.text: Enhanced driving assessment required including wheelchair exercise
Must produce both pass certificates (standard and enhanced)

english_proficiency.text: English proficiency test pass required OR equivalent educational qualification
Exemptions: GCSE in English language or literature, degree with substantial English content, NVQ or BTEC requiring good English communication
Critical for understanding written documents on child and vulnerable adult protection
Oral proficiency relevant to identifying exploitation through passenger communication

right_to_work.text: Right to work check under Immigration Act 2016 required
Proof of eligibility to work in UK with appropriate evidence required

foreign_nationals.text: DBS Enhanced Criminal Convictions Certificate required
Criminal record disclosure from all countries where resided from age 18
Certificate of good conduct from Embassy/Diplomatic Mission if country does not provide criminal record disclosure
If in foreign language: must be accompanied by original certified translation from sworn translator
All translation costs paid by applicant
Certificate of good character from each country of residence (living there longer than 6 months in given calendar year)
Non-English language certificates must be accompanied by translation from Embassy or Consulate of that country
Exception: refugees granted asylum in UK (exception applies only to that country)
Student Visa holders not accepted for operator applications

hackney_carriage_knowledge_test.text: Examination of knowledge regarding rules, regulations, and topographical knowledge of licensing area
Must be passed before licence granted

private_hire_literacy_numeracy_test.text: Covers basic map reading, Rule Book contents, fare calculations, Road Traffic Regulations
Re-sits allowed up to twice within 12 months
After 3 failures: applicant must demonstrate improved skills through Adult Education Department before re-sitting

private_hire_specific.text: Operator must sign application form confirming driver will work for them

duration.text: Hackney Carriage driver licence duration: 3 years
Private Hire driver licence duration: 3 years

renewal_documentation.text: Proof of right to work in UK
All supporting documentation must be received before renewal processed

timing.text: Application must be submitted before expiry date
Council sends reminder 6 weeks before expiry
Submit at least 14 days prior to expiry
No grace period; after expiry, operator must cease taking bookings for fleet vehicles
Licence renewed after expiry date runs from new issue date

arrest_consequences.text: Arrest for notifiable offences likely to result in review of whether licence holder is fit to continue

mandatory_notification.text: Drivers must notify Licensing Authority within 48 hours of arrest and release
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority within 48 hours of charge
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority of conviction for sexual offence
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority of conviction for offence involving dishonesty or violence
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority of conviction for motoring offence
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority of fixed penalty notices
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority of cautions
Drivers must notify Licensing Authority of any change in personal details

nr3_register.text: Licensing Authority checks NR3 register before granting licence
Licensing Authority reports licence refusals and revocations to NR3
Hackney carriage and private hire applicants and licensees must declare whether licence refused or revoked by another authority
All applications must be checked against NR3 database
Decisions to refuse or revoke related to risk of harm to child or vulnerable adult must be referred to DBS and Gloucestershire Constabulary

suitability_section_61.text: Licensing Authority may suspend, revoke, or refuse to renew driver licence if conviction involves dishonesty, indecency, or violence since grant of licence

Licensing Authority may suspend, revoke, or refuse to renew driver licence if conviction under or failure to comply with Town Police Clauses Act 1847 or Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1976 since grant of licence
Licensing Authority may suspend, revoke, or refuse to renew driver licence for any other reasonable cause
Each case considered on individual merits
Council may depart substantially from policy only with clear and compelling reasons
Aim is not to punish twice for offence already subject to criminal action, but to ensure public safety not compromised

badge_requirements.text: Driver must return badge to Licensing Authority upon licence expiry
Driver must return badge to Licensing Authority upon licence suspension or revocation
Driver must return badge to Licensing Authority upon end of employment

code_of_conduct.text: Must behave in civil and orderly manner
Unsatisfactory behaviour or conduct to customers, other road users, Licensing Officers and elected members is breach of conditions
Must meet standards of dress and appearance
Must provide reasonable assistance with loading/unloading of luggage
Must offer reasonable assistance to passengers with disability
Must attend punctually at appointed time and place without reasonable cause

page.summary: Factors and approach for assessing relevance of criminal history to taxi licensing
page.title: Convictions Relevance Policy

legal_basis.policy_based_on: Statutory Taxi and Private Hire Vehicle Standards (Department of Transport, July 2020)

general_principles.text: Policy applies to all new applications for taxi or private hire driver licence
Policy applies to all licensed taxi and private hire drivers
Applied to vehicle licence applicants and holders
Applied to private hire operator applicants and holders not also holding driver licence
Taxi legislation identifies offences involving dishonesty, indecency or violence as concern when assessing fitness
Each case considered on own merits
Applicants/licensees entitled to fair and impartial consideration
Appropriate weight given to evidence provided, including assessing risk of re-offending and harm

factors_for_consideration.text: Age and circumstances when offence committed
History of complaints to authority (unproven complaints may be considered if strong likelihood justified)
Whether applicant intentionally misled authority or lied in application
Information from other agencies or services (Police, Safeguarding)

conviction_treatment.text: Attempt or conspiracy regarded as substantive crime
Fixed penalties and community resolutions considered same as conviction
Hackney carriage and private hire drivers exempt from Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (no spent convictions except protected convictions and protected cautions)

non_conviction_matters.text: Matters not resulting in criminal conviction can and will be considered
non_conviction_matters.examples: acquittal, conviction quashed, decision not to prosecute, continuing investigation with bail

non_conviction_matters.text: Complaints with no police involvement will be considered
Reference to conviction includes criminal behaviour not resulting in conviction

applicant_awaiting_trial.text: Determination deferred until trial completed or charges withdrawn
Existing licensee charged: authority decides action

aggravating_features.text: Offences committed whilst driving hackney carriage or private hire vehicle viewed as aggravating
Unacceptable behaviour whilst driving hackney carriage or private hire vehicle viewed as aggravating
Concerning use in connection with operator viewed as aggravating
Other offences not connected with taxi trades not seen as mitigating factors

pattern_of_behaviour.text: Pattern of offending viewed as more serious than isolated offences

criminality_checks.text: Licensed vehicle proprietors subject to annual basic DBS disclosure check
Exception: proprietors also licensed as hackney carriage or private hire driver with same authority
Fitness determined based on common licensing standards definition and relevance of convictions policy
If cease to hold driver licence, basic certificate required immediately

fitness_assessment.text: Decision on fitness of vehicle proprietor made independent of driver licence refusal
Based on appropriate information and individual circumstances
Important consideration is information from enhanced DBS check but information disclosed on basic check
DBS certificate information only for specific requested purpose and with applicant's full consent

licence_refusal_impact.text: Refusal to licence individual as hackney carriage or private hire driver does not automatically prevent vehicle licence
Suspension or revocation of driver licence does not automatically prevent vehicle licence

page.summary: Criminality checks and fitness assessment for licensed vehicle proprietors
page.title: Vehicle Proprietor Licensing

page.summary: Operator licensing requirements, criminality checks, and record-keeping obligations
page.title: Private Hire Operator Licensing

licence_duration.text: Individual authorities retain right to issue shorter licences in exceptional circumstances

criminality_checks.text: Licensed private hire operators subject to annual basic DBS disclosure check
Exception: operators also licensed as hackney carriage or private hire driver with same authority
Fitness determined based on common licensing standards definition and relevance of convictions policy
If cease to hold driver licence, basic certificate required immediately

company_or_partnership_operators.text: Licence may be applied for by company or partnership
Fit and proper test applies to each director or partner
Must advise licensing authority of any change in directors or partners
Any change requires additional criminality checks

fitness_assessment.text: Decision on fitness of operator made independent of driver licence refusal
Based on appropriate information and individual circumstances
Important consideration is information from enhanced DBS check but information disclosed on basic check
DBS certificate information only for specific requested purpose and with applicant's full consent

record_keeping.text: Licensed private hire operators must record name of passenger for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record time of request for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record pick-up point for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record destination for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record name of driver for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record driver's licence number for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record vehicle registration number for each booking
Licensed private hire operators must record name of individual responding to booking request
Licensed private hire operators must record name of individual dispatching vehicle

booking_and_dispatch_staff.text: All booking and dispatch staff must have basic DBS check
Private hire operators must ensure written policies and procedures in place for criminality checks on all booking and dispatch staff
Private hire operators must ensure written policies on approach on employing ex-offenders
If booking and dispatch function is outsourced, operator must ensure third-party has adequate arrangements

sub_contracting.text: Private Hire operator may take booking and arrange for another licensed operator to make journey

page.summary: Driver obligations for passengers with disabilities and wheelchair accessibility requirements
page.title: Wheelchair Accessible Vehicles & Disability Duties

acts.text: Taxis and Private Hire Vehicles (Disabled Persons) Act 2022

scope.text: Applies to private hire vehicle drivers

driver_training.text: Enhanced driving assessment required for wheelchair accessible vehicle drivers
Must produce both pass certificates (standard and enhanced)

wheelchair_user_obligations.text: Drivers must carry passenger whilst in their wheelchair if they wish
Drivers must carry wheelchair if passenger chooses to sit in passenger seat
Drivers must ensure passenger carried in safety and reasonable comfort
Drivers must give passenger reasonable mobility assistance

mobility_assistance_details.text: Helping passenger get in or out of vehicle
Helping passenger get in or out of vehicle whilst in wheelchair if passenger chooses to remain in wheelchair
Loading wheelchair into or out of vehicle if required

visually_impaired_passengers.text: Drivers must assist visually impaired passengers to identify or find vehicle

charging_restrictions.text: Drivers must not make any extra charge for carrying passenger with wheelchair
Drivers must not make any extra charge for assisting passenger with wheelchair
Meter must not be left running whilst helping wheelchair user
Meter must not be left running whilst passenger gets into or out of vehicle

exemptions.text: Drivers may claim exemption on medical grounds
Drivers may claim exemption because physical condition makes compliance impossible or unreasonably difficult
Exemption must be evidenced by exemption certificate
Exemption certificate must be displayed in vehicle

age_based_requirements.text: Under 3 years old, front seat: appropriate child restraint MUST be used
Under 3 years old, rear seat: appropriate child restraint MUST be used IF AVAILABLE
3–11 years old and under 1.35 metres, front seat: appropriate child restraint MUST be worn IF AVAILABLE; otherwise adult seat belt MUST be worn IF AVAILABLE
3–11 years old and under 1.35 metres, rear seat: appropriate child restraint MUST be used IF AVAILABLE
12–13 years old or 1.35 metres or more in height, front or rear seat: adult seat belt MUST be worn IF AVAILABLE
14 years and over, front or rear seat: adult seat belt MUST be worn IF AVAILABLE

driver_responsibility.text: Driver is responsible for ensuring all passengers over 14 years are strapped in

consequences.text: Potential impact on motor insurance claims
Risk of death or serious injury

penalties.text: Failure to wear seat belt (driver or passenger)
Driver failure to ensure child passenger uses appropriate restraint or seat belt

general_requirements.text: Front seat: all occupants must use appropriate child restraint or adult seat belt if available
Rear seat: all occupants must use appropriate child restraint or adult seat belt if available

page.summary: Mandatory requirements for child and adult seat belt use in taxis and private hire cars
page.title: Child Restraint & Seat Belt Requirements

page.summary: Process and requirements for reporting issues about taxi or private hire drivers and vehicles
page.title: Complain About a Taxi Driver or Vehicle

incident_details.text: Full description of issue or incident

complaint_display_requirements.text: Licensees must display complaint guidance in licensed vehicles
Guidance must be displayed where easily read
Signage must include licensing authority name
Signage must include contact email and address
Signage must include vehicle plate number
Signage must include licensing authority website address

failure_to_display.text: Failure to display guidance results in suspension of vehicle licence
Failure to display guidance may result in review of driving licence

complaint_processing.text: Appropriate action will be taken in accordance with Enforcement and Complaints policy

assessment.text: Licensing Officers assess whether complaint is relevant
Licensing Officers assess whether complaint is frivolous
Licensing Officers assess whether complaint is vexatious
Licensing Officers assess other grounds for dismissal

circumstances.text: Other circumstances deemed sufficiently serious requiring immediate action

page.summary: Triggers and circumstances warranting immediate suspension or revocation action
page.title: Immediate Suspension and Revocation of Licences

trigger_conditions.text: Fitness of licence holder called into question
Raises concerns about whether licence holder should continue to operate
Decision cannot wait until next scheduled Licensing Sub-Committee

ancillary_policies_required.text: Whistleblowing policy and procedure for raising concerns about licensing matters
Data sharing agreements and processes to enable information sharing with other licensing authorities
Procedure(s) for immediate suspension and revocation of licences

enforcement_measures.text: Common Enforcement and Complaints Policy & Procedure
Joint authorisation of enforcement officers across adopting authorities
Information sharing agreements between adopting authorities

impact_on_existing_licences.text: Licences already issued must be reviewed in accordance with statutory guidance on case-by-case basis following policy changes

page.summary: Policy review requirements, ancillary policies, and enforcement measures
page.title: Licensing Policy & Governance

policy_review.text: Licensing policy documents must be reviewed every five years
Licensing policy documents must be reviewed more frequently if significant issues arise
Policy changes must be consulted on with licensed trade and key stakeholders

applicant_rights.text: Applicant/licence holder has right to be represented

page.summary: Rights, powers, and procedures for licensing committee hearings
page.title: Licensing Committee Procedures

appeal_procedures.text: Council must hear appeals in accordance with statutory procedures

page.summary: Appeal procedures and authority requirements
page.title: Rights of Appeal

page.summary: Current licensing fees for 2026 to 2027
page.title: Hackney Carriage & Private Hire Fees

driver_fees.licence_category: Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Driver
Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Driver
Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Driver
Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Driver

vehicle_fees.licence_category: Hackney Carriage and Private Hire Vehicle

additional_vehicle_fees.fee_category: Replacement of Interior Plate or Front Exterior Plate
Copy or Replacement of Licence Certificate
Application to Notify Change of Address

notes.text: Operators who are not licensed Private Hire or Hackney Carriage drivers must also apply for a basic DBS check

page.summary: Approved taxi rank locations and capacity in Gloucester
page.title: Taxi Ranks (Hackney Carriage Stands)

page.summary: List of approved vehicle testing stations for hackney carriage and private hire vehicles
page.title: Approved Hackney Carriage & Private Hire Testing Stations

stations.address: Unit 14/15 Severnside Trading Estate, Sudmeadow Road

page.summary: Regulated taxi fares and tariff information
page.title: Hackney Carriage Fares - Gloucester City Council

fare_rates.description: Between 9:00pm and 7:00am Monday to Saturday, all day Sundays and between 6:00pm and 11:00pm on Xmas Eve and New Year's Eve

Between 11:00pm on the day before and 7:00am on the morning following each and every PUBLIC HOLIDAY; Good Friday, Easter Sunday, Easter Monday, Early May Bank Holiday, Spring Bank Holiday, Summer Bank Holiday, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, and New Year's Day

additional_charges.description: Each passenger over ONE per journey
Each passenger over FOUR per journey

general.text: Carriage of packages, bicycles, prams, animals, intoxicated or troublesome persons is solely at driver's discretion
Neither owner nor driver accepts any responsibility for passengers over 14 years of age who fail to fasten their seat belts
FROM 6AM ON 1 JULY 2007 ALL LICENSED VEHICLES BECAME SMOKEFREE

receipts.text: These are available showing taxi and driver's licence numbers and journey details. OBTAIN ONE IN THE EVENT OF A DISPUTE

telephone_hiring.text: Unless contract of hire is made with or through the driver of the hackney carriage in which passenger is carried and made at a time when hackney carriage is plying or waiting for hire at appointed stand, driver is not permitted to engage meter until hackney carriage arrives at point from which passenger is to be picked up

page.summary: List of licensed wheelchair accessible hackney carriage and private hire vehicles
page.title: Designated Wheelchair Accessible Vehicles
